aniimoBetaDownload.title: Aniimo Global Closed Beta Download Guide
aniimoBetaDownload.subtitle: Check your beta access, install Aniimo on an eligible device, and prepare your account before entering Idyll.
aniimoBetaDownload.intro: The Global Closed Beta opened on July 9, 2026 for the official PC launcher, Steam, Android, and iOS. It is a limited-access, free, non-charging test, and all test progress is reset when the beta ends.

steps.title: Check the Global Beta Schedule
steps.description: Pre-download opened on July 8, 2026, with staggered server starts across regions.
points: America server start: July 8 at 22:00 UTC-4.
Europe server start: July 9 at 02:00 UTC+0.
APAC server start: July 9 at 10:00 UTC+8.

steps.title: Confirm Your Beta Access
steps.description: Verify eligibility through the official Aniimo website before installing.
points: Open the Aniimo website and select Log in to Check or Check Beta Access.
Use the same email address submitted in the beta application.
Check the Junk or Spam folder if the confirmation email is missing.

steps.title: Install the Official PC Version
steps.description: Eligible PC testers use the official Aniimo launcher.
points: Select PC Download on the website or the link in the eligibility email.
Open the Aniimo launcher and select Install to download the game files.
Always start Aniimo through the official launcher.

steps.title: Activate the Steam Version
steps.description: Steam testers redeem the unique product code from the invitation email.
points: Open Steam, select Games, then Activate a Product on Steam.
Enter the product code from the beta invitation email.
Install Aniimo and sign in with the email that received the code.

steps.title: Install Aniimo on iOS and Android
steps.description: Mobile testers receive platform-specific install instructions.
points: iOS testers get a TestFlight invitation and redemption code by email.
Android testers use the official Android Download link after confirming access.
Sign in with the same email used for the beta application.

steps.title: Check Storage and Device Requirements
steps.description: Make sure your device meets the listed specifications before installing.
points: PC: 45 GB free, i7-9700 or Ryzen 5 3600X, GTX 1060 6 GB, 12 GB RAM, Windows 10 64-bit.
iOS: 25 GB free, iPhone 12 Pro with iOS 16 and 6 GB RAM.
Android: 25 GB free, Snapdragon 7 Gen 2 or Dimensity 9000, Android 10, 6 GB RAM.

steps.title: Fix Common Access and Performance Problems
steps.description: Resolve the most frequent launch and login issues quickly.
points: Missing email: check the application inbox and Junk folder, then contact official support.
Mobile lag or heating: close background apps, restart, and lower graphics settings.
Unstable connection: use 5 GHz Wi-Fi or a wired connection and avoid proxies or VPNs.
Character stuck in terrain: open the menu with Esc and select Unstuck.

steps.title: Understand Account and Data-Wipe Rules
steps.description: Know how cross-platform progress and test data are handled.
points: All beta progress and account data are deleted when the Global Closed Beta ends.
Beta access cannot be transferred, shared, sold, rented, or gifted.
iOS or Android access works on PC too, keeping cross-platform progress during the test.

aniimoReleasePlatforms.title: Aniimo Release Date, Platforms, and Price
aniimoReleasePlatforms.subtitle: Aniimo is scheduled to launch worldwide in Q3 2026 as a free-to-play creature-catching open-world RPG.
aniimoReleasePlatforms.intro: Pawprint Studio has announced console, PC, and mobile versions of Aniimo. Players will explore Idyll, collect magical creatures, build teams, and use the Twine system to control an Aniimo directly during exploration and combat.

aniimoBeginnerGuide.title: Aniimo Beginner Guide: Your First Session in Idyll
aniimoBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Follow the main route, assemble a balanced roster, collect Aniipods, and unlock useful exploration abilities without missing early rewards.
aniimoBeginnerGuide.intro: Aniimo combines open-world exploration, real-time combat, creature capture, elemental counters, quests, and Twine. A strong first session focuses on the main story while building a small roster that covers damage, BREAK, support, and multiple elements.

steps.title: Follow the Main Quest First
steps.description: Use the mission tracker to unlock Aniimo's core systems before branching out.
points: Complete tutorial prompts so Aniimo Management, Twine, capture tools, and shops unlock.
If a quest NPC disappears, teleport away and return or restart the game.

steps.title: Create Your Active Aniimo Roster
steps.description: A balanced team covers damage, BREAK, support, and several elements.
points: Open Aniimo Management with Esc and drag Aniimo into the team slots.
Mix elements instead of stacking one damage type.
Add a BREAK-focused Aniimo and a support or healing option.

steps.title: Learn the Essential PC Controls
steps.description: A few keybinds unlock movement, menus, and combat control.
points: M opens the world map; Esc opens the menu and Aniimo Management.
C activates Twine for direct Aniimo control; H switches rosters.
Esc then Unstuck frees a character trapped in terrain.

steps.title: Explore with the Right Mobility Ability
steps.description: Twine unlocks creature-specific movement for every terrain.
points: Mobility skills include higher jumps, aerial dashes, climbing, flying, swimming, and tunneling.
Use elemental abilities to interact with puzzles and reach hidden rewards.
Open the map with M to find unexplored habitats.

steps.title: Level a Focused Group of Aniimo
steps.description: Concentrate resources on a core team before spreading upgrades.
points: Active roster members earn experience from enemies defeated in Twine mode.
Use Experience Gems when a key member needs immediate levels.
Build a core team before investing in every captured Aniimo.

steps.title: Collect and Restock Aniipods
steps.description: Keep capture tools stocked from multiple reliable sources.
points: Find Aniipods in exploration loot, treasure chests, story quests, and Aniilog objectives.
Purchase them from Outpost or Astra shops.
Produce more with the Aniipod Maker in the Homeland.

steps.title: Increase Your Capture Chance
steps.description: Preparation beats raw luck when catching valuable Aniimo.
points: Approach sleeping Aniimo or use stealth before a capture.
Deal BREAK damage to weaken the target during combat.
Raise the Researcher Level to unlock capture-enhancing skills.

steps.title: Use Elemental Counters
steps.description: The Type Matchup panel turns elements into a combat advantage.
points: Open Aniimo Management and choose an element icon to view matchups.
Rotate team members when an enemy resists your main attacker.
Maintain fire, water, grass, electric, rock, wind, dark, ice, and holy coverage.

steps.title: Claim Limited and Daily Rewards
steps.description: Log in and clear objectives before each reset.
points: Complete the Global Closed Beta daily check-in event for cumulative rewards.
Check-ins refresh each day at 04:00 UTC+8.
Finish daily quests and event objectives before they reset.

quickTips: Do not rely on a single element against every enemy.
Use stealth, BREAK damage, or a stronger Aniipod before difficult captures.
Keep mobility Aniimo ready, since many routes and rewards need specialized movement.
Save Experience Gems for active team members, and never miss daily check-ins.

aniimoTierList.title: Aniimo Tier List: Best Creatures for an Early Roster
aniimoTierList.subtitle: These Global Closed Beta picks combine combat performance, elemental coverage, support value, and useful exploration abilities.
aniimoTierList.intro: A practical early team includes a main attacker, a BREAK specialist, a support option, and several movement abilities. The picks below favor basic forms that deliver immediate value across combat and exploration.

creatures.name: Nimbi
creatures.combat: Cloud Shield absorbs damage and reduces stamina consumption while ranged skills add safe damage.
creatures.exploration: Puff Up raises jump height and adds an airborne dash, plus Fly Lv.1 pathfinding.

creatures.name: Budclaw
creatures.combat: High BREAK and physical defense make Budclaw effective against durable enemies.
creatures.exploration: Tunnel moves underground and grants immunity to ranged attacks while active.

creatures.name: Celestis
creatures.combat: A ranged magic attacker with strong base ATK, Shooting Star Bombs, and Star Glide.
creatures.exploration: Provides both Climb Lv.1 and Fly Lv.1 pathfinding for broad exploration.

creatures.name: Emberpup
creatures.combat: Fire Kick rewards dodges with extra damage, and Fire Bolt applies a ranged Fire debuff.
creatures.exploration: Hustle increases movement speed while consuming stamina.

creatures.name: Dewy
creatures.combat: Withering Bloom damages nearby enemies and restores team HP, while lowering Dark resistance.
creatures.exploration: Provides Fly Lv.1 pathfinding for aerial routes.

creatures.name: Fentuft
creatures.combat: Thunder Field adds extra lightning strikes and Lightning Dart pulls targets together.
creatures.exploration: Scurry gives a short forward rush alongside Fly Lv.1 pathfinding.

creatures.name: Shelly
creatures.combat: Hydro Roll deals high physical damage, creates a puddle, and triggers Water damage bonuses.
creatures.exploration: Hydro Roll provides fast forward movement while Twined.

creatures.name: Sparki
creatures.combat: Fire Orb adds aimed ranged damage and Trick Fire Ring repeatedly applies Fire debuff.
creatures.exploration: Chosen for ranged combat and Fire interactions over specialized movement.

aniimoCatchingTwine.title: Aniimo Catching and Twine Guide
aniimoCatchingTwine.subtitle: Improve captures with stealth, BREAK damage, stronger Aniipods, and careful observation, then Twine with Aniimo to transform, explore, solve puzzles, and fight directly.
aniimoCatchingTwine.intro: Catching an Aniimo is more than throwing an Aniipod. Study wild behavior, weaken resistant creatures with BREAK damage, use traps and the environment, and choose a suitable Aniipod. Twine Mode then lets you experience Idyll through that Aniimo's movement and combat abilities.

items.title: Prepare the Capture
items.summary: Observe the target before starting combat or using an Aniipod.
details: Wild Aniimo have different behaviors, habitats, and reactions to nearby Pathfinders.
Sleeping Aniimo are easier targets, while alert creatures can be approached with stealth.
Use the map to explore different regions when searching for undiscovered species.

items.title: Use Stealth, Traps, and the Environment
items.summary: Avoid turning every encounter into a direct fight.
details: Approach quietly when terrain and creature behavior allow it.
Set traps and use environmental features to create a better capture window.
Watch movement patterns before committing resources.

items.title: Apply BREAK Damage
items.summary: BREAK damage creates a stronger capture opportunity during combat.
details: Use attacks that reduce the target's resistance before throwing an Aniipod.
Avoid defeating the Aniimo before the capture attempt.
Combine elemental pressure, BREAK damage, and timing against stronger targets.

items.title: Choose the Right Aniipod
items.summary: Higher-tier Aniipods provide better capture support than basic options.
details: Aniipods come from loot, chests, story quests, Aniilog objectives, and Training rewards.
Additional Aniipods are sold through Outpost and Astra shops.
The Aniipod Maker inside the Homeland provides another production option.

items.title: Raise Researcher Level
items.summary: Research progression unlocks skills that improve capture performance.
details: Complete exploration, research, photography, and capture activities.
Use capture-enhancing skills when pursuing rare Aniimo.
Save stronger Aniipods for targets that stay difficult after preparation.

items.title: Track Weather and Time of Day
items.summary: The living ecosystem changes which Aniimo appear and how encounters play out.
details: Nocturnal Aniimo emerge after dark, making night exploration essential for some finds.
Weather alters combat conditions, such as rain weakening fire abilities.
Revisit habitats under different weather and lighting when a target is missing.

items.title: Use Twine for Exploration
items.summary: Twining transforms you into a collected Aniimo and grants its movement abilities.
details: Different Aniimo climb cliffs, glide, dive underwater, or reach hidden areas.
Some transformations let you understand Aniimo language and observe their society.
Switch transformations when a route, collectible, or puzzle needs different movement.

items.title: Use Twine in Puzzles and Combat
items.summary: Twine Mode supports physics-based puzzles and direct real-time battles.
details: Combine Aniimo abilities with fire, water, and wind to operate mechanisms.
In Twine Mode you directly control the Aniimo, dodge, perform combos, and use skills.
Command Mode stays available when tactical roster control is more useful.

aniimoEvolutionProgression.title: Aniimo Evolution and Progression Guide
aniimoEvolutionProgression.subtitle: Level your core companions, collect evolution resources, evaluate traits and personalities, and delay expensive upgrades until your main roster is set.
aniimoEvolutionProgression.intro: Aniimo progression combines direct leveling, combat experience, branching evolution, personalities, traits, ability values, and collectible forms. Experience Gems give immediate levels, while active-roster Aniimo earn experience from enemies defeated during Twine combat.

stages.method: Pick a small group covering exploration, combat, BREAK pressure, and multiple elements.
Place Aniimo in the active roster and defeat enemies in Twine Mode.
Apply Experience Gems directly to a selected Aniimo for instant levels.
Release an upgraded Aniimo when it no longer fits the roster.
Collect evolution materials before committing to a branch.
Advance eligible Aniimo through their multi-branch evolution routes.
Compare captured Aniimo before making a large investment.
stages.resources: Personality, stats, traits, Trait Switch Potion, Potential Fruit

stages.method: Join form-specific capture, hatching, cultivation, and competitive activities.
stages.resources: Prismana Eggs, Shimmering Eggs, and special-mode rewards

stages.method: Complete Holo-Battle Sim challenges and resource events.
stages.resources: Resonators, Pure Luminite, Experience Gems, event rewards

aniimoCombatTeams.title: Aniimo Combat, Elements, and Team Builds
aniimoCombatTeams.subtitle: Combine tactical commands with direct Twine combat, cover multiple elemental matchups, and prepare roster structures for exploration, Alpha bosses, Holo-Battle, and challenging encounters.
aniimoCombatTeams.intro: Aniimo uses two connected combat styles. Command Mode directs Aniimo and coordinates skills, while Twine Mode transforms you into an Aniimo for real-time movement, dodging, attacks, and combos. Switch prepared roster members and review elemental matchups through Aniimo Management.

items.title: Command Mode
items.setup: Keep complementary Aniimo in the active roster and issue commands by enemy behavior.
items.playstyle: Use it when positioning, coordinated abilities, or safe damage delivery matter most.

items.title: Twine Mode
items.setup: Twine with an active Aniimo to use its movement, attacks, dodges, and skills.
items.playstyle: Best for reacting to boss attacks, executing combos, and applying BREAK pressure.

items.title: Elemental Coverage
items.setup: Build around multiple elements instead of one damage type.
items.playstyle: Check the Type Matchup panel and switch to an effective element before hard content.

items.title: Roster Switching
items.useCase: Changing roles or elements during combat
items.setup: Build rosters in Aniimo Management, use C to Twine, and H to switch members.
items.playstyle: Open with the best matchup, then switch for stronger BREAK, mobility, or support.

items.title: Balanced Exploration Team
items.useCase: Quests, gathering, puzzles, and regular encounters
items.setup: One mobility Twine option, one dependable damage dealer, and one flexible Aniimo.
items.playstyle: Prioritize movement variety and broad coverage for terrain and unplanned fights.

items.title: Alpha Boss Team
items.setup: One counter-element attacker, one strong BREAK Aniimo, and one durable support.
items.playstyle: Learn the pattern, BREAK for an opening, and save Twine attacks for weak phases.

items.title: Holo-Battle Team
items.setup: A primary counter-element dealer, a secondary attacker, and a consistency utility pick.
items.playstyle: Adjust the roster for each simulation instead of forcing one universal team.

items.title: Difficult Encounter Team
items.useCase: Unknown enemies, long fights, and mixed groups
items.setup: Two different elemental attackers plus a flexible third for BREAK, recovery, or control.
items.playstyle: Start cautious in Command Mode, then Twine with the member that fits each phase.

aniimoOpenWorld.title: Aniimo Open World, Bosses, and Multiplayer
aniimoOpenWorld.subtitle: Travel through varied regions, solve cooperative Sanctums, fight Alpha Aniimo, compete in Diamond Egg Heist, exchange creatures, and meet other Pathfinders.
aniimoOpenWorld.intro: The continent of Idyll combines open-world exploration with puzzles, real-time challenges, competitive activities, cooperative bosses, player housing, and social hubs. Twine changes how you move through each environment and lets different Aniimo abilities open new routes and puzzle solutions.

items.title: The Continent of Idyll
items.location: Forests, volcanic terrain, floating cities, coastlines, and underwater areas
items.activity: Capture Aniimo, complete quests, discover treasure, race, climb, glide, dive, and solve physics puzzles.
items.multiplayer: Solo exploration with online social and multiplayer systems throughout the world.

items.title: Astra and the Homeland
items.location: Astra and the player's RV-connected Homeland
items.activity: Buy accessories, meet Pathfinders, customize the Homeland, grow crops, and interact with owned Aniimo.
items.multiplayer: Astra is a busy social area, while RV campsites and Homelands support visits and shared activities.

items.title: Island of Peace
items.location: A newly introduced area within Idyll
items.activity: Explore aquatic environments, discover wild Aniimo and forms, and use water-oriented movement.
items.multiplayer: Includes cooperative boss challenges against Bubbask and Coraliz.

items.title: Alpha Aniimo
items.activity: Fight stronger Aniimo with large health pools, dangerous patterns, and BREAK opportunities.
items.multiplayer: Selected Alpha encounters can be challenged with other Pathfinders.

items.title: Diamond Egg Heist
items.activity: Up to six teams of three race to Twine with the diamond egg and escort it to safety.
items.multiplayer: Team-based mode blending exploration, combat, objective control, and escort play.

items.title: Holo-Battle Interlink
items.activity: Defeat Alpha Aniimo, earn points, complete combat challenges, and collect rewards.
items.multiplayer: Challenges can be attempted alone or with friends.

items.title: Sanctum Puzzles
items.location: Sanctums and puzzle dungeons like Sanctum: Rigged Maze
items.activity: Twine with complementary Aniimo, operate mechanisms, and solve environmental puzzles.
items.multiplayer: Two-player co-op is supported for Sanctum puzzle challenges.

items.title: Aniimo Exchange & Social Play
items.location: Online social systems and player hubs
items.activity: Exchange Aniimo, form teams, socialize in urban hubs, and join cooperative or competitive activities.
items.multiplayer: Steam lists online co-op, online PvP, and cross-platform multiplayer support.

items.title: Named Test Regions
items.location: Astra, Tideblossom Coast, Rosetower Wood, Russet Highlands, Lost Isles, and Island of Peace
items.activity: Search each region for different Aniimo, conditions, quests, treasures, bosses, and puzzle routes.
items.multiplayer: Activities range from solo discovery to co-op bosses and team-based PvP.

faq.title: Aniimo
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Aniimo, the Global Closed Beta, and the worldwide launch.

questions.question: What is Aniimo?
questions.answer: Aniimo is a free-to-play, open-world creature-catching RPG from Pawprint Studio. You catch and evolve magical Aniimo, Twine with them to explore Idyll, solve puzzles, battle in real time, and build a Homeland.

questions.question: When does Aniimo release?
questions.answer: Aniimo is scheduled to launch worldwide in Q3 2026 as a free-to-play game across PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, iOS, and Android.

questions.question: Which platforms is Aniimo on?
questions.answer: Aniimo is coming to PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, Steam, the Epic Games Store, Xbox PC, Android, and iOS. You can wishlist Aniimo now on the available stores.

questions.question: Is Aniimo free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Aniimo is free-to-play on every launch platform. The Global Closed Beta is also free and does not charge testers.

questions.question: What is Twine in Aniimo?
questions.answer: Twine is Aniimo's signature system. It transforms you into a collected Aniimo so you can use its movement, exploration, puzzle, and real-time combat abilities directly.

questions.question: Does Aniimo have multiplayer and PvP?
questions.answer: Yes. Aniimo supports online co-op, cross-platform multiplayer, social hubs, and competitive modes such as the team-based Diamond Egg Heist in the Lost Isles.

questions.question: How do I join the Aniimo Global Closed Beta?
questions.answer: Check your eligibility on the official Aniimo website with the email used in your application. Selected testers receive platform install instructions by email for PC, Steam, iOS, and Android.

questions.question: How many elements does Aniimo have?
questions.answer: Aniimo creatures use nine elements: fire, water, grass, electric, rock, wind, dark, ice, and holy. Elemental counters are central to combat and team building.

cta.title: Ready to Explore Aniimo?
cta.description: Join the Aniimo community on Discord for guides, beta updates, and discussions. Wishlist on Steam and get ready for the Q3 2026 global launch!
